title: podocarpus macrophyllus subsp. piliramulus
intro: Podocarpus macrophyllus subsp. piliramulus, also known as the Chinese Plum Yew, is a plant species belonging to the cypress family (Podocarpaceae). The leaves of the Chinese Plum Yew have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of the Chinese Plum Yew are often made into teas, infusions, or used in traditional Chinese medicine formulas to harness its medicinal properties.
